name: Web research agent — citation-aware eval set
description: Three factual questions answerable from Wikipedia. The expected final_response is a concrete golden example; the LLM judge handles natural-language variation. Expected tool_uses reference only tool names (Wikipedia article titles are stable enough that arg matching would also work, but we keep args sparse for consistency with the k8s eval sets).

parts.text: Who founded Anthropic and what year was the company founded?
Anthropic was founded in 2021 by Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei, both former OpenAI executives (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthropic).
Who created the Linux kernel and in what year was it first released?
Linus Torvalds released the first version of the Linux kernel in 1991 while he was a student at the University of Helsinki (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linux_kernel).
What is OpenTelemetry and what is its current CNCF maturity level?

OpenTelemetry is an open-source observability framework for generating, collecting, and exporting telemetry data (traces, metrics, and logs) from cloud-native software (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenTelemetry). It is an incubating project under the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cloud_Native_Computing_Foundation).
